info.title: Unified Trading System API
info.description: Comprehensive API spec generated from all FastAPI services in the Unified Trading System. Each service is represented as a tag group with its endpoints prefixed by /{service-name}/.

get.description:
List all available services with their sharding configurations.

Returns:
    List of services with their descriptions and dimensions.

Get detailed configuration for a specific service.

Args:
    service_name: Name of the service

Returns:
    Full sharding configuration for the service.

Get dimension values for a specific service.

Args:
    service_name: Name of the service

Returns:
    Dimension definitions with their possible values.

Discover config files from a cloud storage path.

This endpoint lists all .json config files in a cloud storage directory
and returns the count for shard estimation.

Args:
    service_name: Name of the service (for validation)
    cloud_path: Cloud storage path (gs://... or s3://...)

Returns:
    List of discovered config file paths and count.

List subdirectories in a cloud storage path.

This enables hierarchical browsing of GCS directories for config selection.

Args:
    service_name: Name of the service (for context)
    cloud_path: Cloud storage path (gs://bucket/prefix/)

Returns:
    List of immediate subdirectory names.

Get the default config bucket(s) for a service.

Returns the GCS bucket path where configs are typically stored.
List deployments with optional filtering and pagination.

schema.title: Response List Deployments Api Deployments Get

post.description: Create a new deployment.

get.description: Get detailed status for a specific deployment.

schema.title: Response Get Deployment Status Api Deployments  Deployment Id  Get
Response Update Deployment Api Deployments  Deployment Id  Patch

delete.description: Delete a deployment and its resources.

schema.title: Response Delete Deployment Api Deployments  Deployment Id  Delete

get.description: Verify deployment completion and data integrity.

schema.title: Response Verify Deployment Completion Api Deployments  Deployment Id  Verify Get

post.description: Get quota requirements and recommendations for a deployment.

schema.title: Response Quota Info Api Deployments Quota Info Post

post.description: Cancel a running deployment.

schema.title: Response Cancel Deployment Api Deployments  Deployment Id  Cancel Post

post.description: Resume a cancelled or failed deployment.

schema.title: Response Resume Deployment Api Deployments  Deployment Id  Resume Post

post.description: Retry failed shards in a deployment.

schema.title: Response Retry Failed Shards Api Deployments  Deployment Id  Retry Failed Post

post.description: Delete multiple deployments.

schema.title: Response Bulk Delete Deployments Api Deployments Bulk Delete Post

post.description: Refresh deployment status from cloud provider.

schema.title: Response Refresh Deployment Status Api Deployments  Deployment Id  Refresh Post

get.description: Get deployment logs with filtering options.

parameters.name: log_type

schema.description: Type of logs: all, error, warning
schema.title: Log Type

parameters.description: Type of logs: all, error, warning

schema.title: Response Get Deployment Logs Api Deployments  Deployment Id  Logs Get

get.description: Get a detailed deployment report with analysis and recommendations.

schema.title: Response Get Deployment Report Api Deployments  Deployment Id  Report Get

get.description:
Return the full shard event stream for a deployment.

Events are written by deployment-service backends to GCS as JSONL and aggregated
here. Each event captures a lifecycle step (JOB_STARTED, VM_PREEMPTED, etc.)
with timestamp, message, and optional metadata.

schema.title: Response Get Deployment Events Api Deployments  Deployment Id  Events Get

get.description:
Return VM-level infrastructure events for a deployment.

Filters the full event stream to VM_PREEMPTED, VM_DELETED, VM_QUOTA_EXHAUSTED,
VM_ZONE_UNAVAILABLE, VM_TIMEOUT, CONTAINER_OOM, CLOUD_RUN_REVISION_FAILED.
Used by the History tab to surface infrastructure failure badges on shard rows.

schema.title: Response Get Deployment Vm Events Api Deployments  Deployment Id  Vm Events Get

post.description:
Roll back a live Cloud Run Service deployment to the previous revision.

Only valid for deployments with deploy_mode="live". Calls the deployment-service
LiveDeployer to revert traffic to the specified (or previous) Cloud Run revision.

schema.title: Response Rollback Live Deployment Api Deployments  Deployment Id  Rollback Post

get.description:
Return the current health check status of a live Cloud Run Service.

Polls the service /health endpoint and returns a structured response.
Used by DeploymentDetails to show a live health badge for live-mode deployments.

schema.title: Response Get Live Deployment Health Api Deployments  Deployment Id  Live Health Get

get.description:
Return the configured GCS region for UI validation.

Used by DeployForm and DataStatusTab to warn when user selects a different region
(cross-region egress costs).

Validate requested deployment region against GCS region.

Returns cross_region flag and warning message when deploying to a different
region than GCS (incurs egress costs).

parameters.name: requested_region

schema.description: Region user wants to deploy to
schema.title: Requested Region

parameters.description: Region user wants to deploy to

get.description:
Get all venues organized by category.

Returns:
    Venues grouped by category (CEFI, TRADFI, DEFI) with their data types.

Get venues for a specific category.

Args:
    category: Category name (CEFI, TRADFI, DEFI)

Returns:
    List of venues and their data types for the category.

Get expected data start dates for all services.

Returns:
    Start dates by service, category, and venue.

Get expected start dates for a specific service.

Args:
    service_name: Name of the service

Returns:
    Start dates by category and venue for the service.

Get service dependency graph.

Returns:
    Full dependencies configuration including execution order.

Get dependencies for a specific service.

Args:
    service_name: Name of the service

Returns:
    Upstream dependencies, downstream dependents, and full DAG context.

Get production readiness checklist for a service.

Reads from unified-trading-codex/10-audit/repos/{service}.yaml (v3.0 schema).

schema.title: Response Get Service Checklist Api Checklists  Service Name  Checklist Get

get.description:
Validate service readiness for deployment.

Returns blocking items and overall readiness. Ready = no blocking items
AND readiness >= 95% (threshold allows in-progress non-critical items).

schema.title: Response Validate Service Checklist Api Checklists  Service Name  Checklist Validate Get

get.description:
List all available service checklists with summary status.

Reads exclusively from codex v3.0 SSOT (unified-trading-codex/10-audit/repos/).

schema.title: Response List Checklists Api Checklists Get

get.description:
List all epics with summary readiness percentages.

Returns compact summaries: epic_id, display_name, mvp_priority,
epic_pct, total_required, completed, epic_complete, blocking_count.

Reads from:
  - codex_dir (10-audit/repos/) for per-repo data
  - epics_dir (11-project-management/epics/) for epic definitions

schema.title: Response List Epics Api Epics Get

get.description:
Get detailed epic status: per-repo CR/BR stages, branch status,
data availability, feature groups, ML models, and blocking reasons.

schema.title: Response Get Epic Detail Api Epics  Epic Id  Get

Returns completion percentages broken down by category and venue,
with optional list of missing dates.

schema.title: Include Dates List

get.description: Get available venue filters for a service.
List files in a specific GCS bucket path.
Get list of instruments for a category.
Check instrument availability over a date range.

post.description: Analyze data patterns and trends for a service.
Get aggregated data status across multiple services.

get.description:
Get comprehensive status for a service.

Returns temporal audit trail with timestamps for:
- Last data update (GCS)
- Last deployment (state files)
- Last build (Cloud Build API)
- Last code push (GitHub API)

Plus anomaly detection.

Get FAST status overview for all services.

Returns a lightweight summary - skips slow GitHub/Build lookups.
For full details, query individual service status.

get.summary: Get Execution Service Data Status Endpoint
get.description: Get execution-service data status by checking configs vs results.

post.description: Calculate missing config x date shards for execution-service.

get.description:
Get API capabilities for UI display.

Includes GCS Fuse status: whether the API is using FUSE mounts for GCS
reads (faster) or falling back to GCS API.

Get supported categories for a service from its sharding config.

Returns the categories that can be used for deployment and data status checks.
This allows the UI to hide irrelevant category filters (e.g., hide CEFI/DEFI
when viewing corporate-actions which is TRADFI-only).

Args:
    service: Service name (e.g., "instruments-service", "corporate-actions")

Returns:
    {"service": str, "categories": List[str]}

Examples:
    - instruments-service: ["CEFI", "DEFI", "TRADFI"]
    - corporate-actions: ["TRADFI"]
    - features-calendar-service: []  # No category dimension

schema.title: Response Get Service Categories Api Capabilities Service Categories  Service  Get

get.description:
List all Cloud Build triggers with their current status.

Returns a list of triggers with last build info.
Results are cached for 5 minutes (TTL_BUILD_INFO) to avoid slow Cloud Build API calls.
Use force_refresh=true to bypass cache.

parameters.name: force_refresh

schema.description: Bypass cache and fetch fresh data
schema.title: Force Refresh

parameters.description: Bypass cache and fetch fresh data

post.description:
Manually trigger a Cloud Build for a service.

This runs the build trigger as if code was pushed to the specified branch.

Requires: roles/cloudbuild.builds.editor on the service account.

get.description:
Get build history for a specific service.

Returns the most recent builds for the service's trigger.

Get detailed status for a library/SDK (like unified-trading-library).

Returns:
- Package version from pyproject.toml
- Latest commit info from GitHub
- Recent build status from Cloud Build
- Which services depend on this library

Check if any libraries have failing quality gates that could affect services.

This is useful for catching version mismatches before they cause runtime errors.

List available builds for a service from Artifact Registry.

Returns builds sorted by version descending. In mock mode (CLOUD_MOCK_MODE=true or
CLOUD_PROVIDER=local), falls back to deployed_versions from the PM manifest.

Build display format: "{version} @ {branch}" — human-readable and idempotent
(same version + branch = same artifact, enforced by immutable AR tags).

parameters.name: env

schema.description: Target environment: dev, staging, or prod
schema.title: Env

parameters.description: Target environment: dev, staging, or prod

schema.title: Response List Builds Api Builds  Service  Get

post.description:
Deploy a specific build tag to any environment.

No version gate — deployment is always a manual decision. Pre-1.0.0 builds
are explicitly allowed for development and testing.

This triggers a Cloud Run revision update with the specified image tag.
In mock mode, returns a dry-run response without calling Cloud Run.

schema.title: Response Deploy Build Api Deployments  Service  Deploy Post

post.description:
Write a new version of the domain config.

Validates the content against the domain schema, writes a versioned YAML
blob to cloud storage, updates the active pointer, and publishes a
config-domain-{domain} event to notify all subscribing services.

parameters.name: domain

schema.description: Config domain: instruments, strategies, clients, venues
schema.title: Domain

parameters.description: Config domain: instruments, strategies, clients, venues

get.description: Read the active domain config from the config store.
List config versions for the domain (newest first, up to 50).

schema.title: Response List Domain Config Versions Api Config Store  Domain  Versions Get

get.description: Return a unified diff between two config versions.

post.description: Roll back domain config to a specific version by re-saving it as the new active.

Trigger pipeline UAT LLM commentary for a completed batch run.

Returns 202 Accepted immediately. Commentary is generated asynchronously
and written to GCS at:
  gs://deployment-store-{project}/pipeline-uat/day={date}/run={run_id}.json

Args:
    request: PipelineUATRequest with job_name, run_id, gcs_manifest_path.
    background_tasks: FastAPI BackgroundTasks for async execution.

Returns:
    Accepted response with run_id and status.

schema.title: Response Pipeline Uat Commentary Api Commentary Pipeline Uat Post

post.description:
Clear all API caches.

This invalidates:
- In-memory cache
- Redis cache (if available)
- GCS cache (if available)

Use this to force fresh data on next request.

get.description: Serve index.html for client-side routing (SPA).

Layer 2 infra health check.

Verifies that required GCS buckets, PubSub topics, and Secret Manager
entries are accessible. Run after Terraform apply, before Layer 3 smoke tests.

Returns:
    JSON with status ("ok" | "degraded" | "error" | "skip"), check details, and errors.

schema.title: Response Infra Health Infra Health Get

List prefixes (folders) within a GCS bucket.

For GCS: provide bucket and optional prefix
For local: provide directory path

List files (not folders) within a GCS bucket prefix.

Returns actual file paths for config selection.
Get suggested local directory for loading results in UI.

Get execution alpha data for a specific backtest run.

Returns entry/exit fills with slippage, equity curve, and summary metrics.

schema.description: Run folder path (GCS prefix or local path)
schema.title: Run Path

parameters.description: Run folder path (GCS prefix or local path)

get.description:
Get full details for a specific result.

Includes orders, fills, equity curve, timeline, and execution alpha.
Get overall performance metrics. Returns mock data when CLOUD_MOCK_MODE=true.

schema.title: Response Get Performance Api V1 Analysis Performance Get

post.description:
Aggregate results by a dimension.

Groups results by algorithm, strategy, timeframe, etc. and
calculates summary statistics for each group.

Get distribution of a metric.

Returns histogram bins and statistics (mean, median, std, etc.)

Get time series of a metric.

Returns cumulative values over time, optionally grouped by date or hour.

Get heatmap data for two dimensions.

Returns a matrix of values for visualization as a heatmap.

get.description:
Get best performing configs.

Ranks configs by the specified metric and returns top N.
List completed backtest results. Returns mock data when CLOUD_MOCK_MODE=true.

post.description:
Run a single backtest.

Accepts a GCS config path and execution parameters.
Returns a job ID for tracking progress.

Example:
    POST /api/v1/backtest/run
    {
        "config_gcs_path": "gs://execution-store-.../configs/V1/.../BENCHMARK_V1.json",  # noqa: gs-uri
        "start_date": "2023-05-23",
        "end_date": "2023-05-24",
        "fast_mode": true
    }

Run batch backtests.

Accepts up to 10 GCS config paths and runs them in parallel.
Returns a batch ID and individual job IDs.

Run a single backtest with local config JSON.

Accepts config content directly (not from GCS).
Useful for running backtests with uploaded/modified configs.

Example:
    POST /api/v1/backtest/run-local
    {
        "config_json": {"config_id": "...", "strategy_id": "...", ...},
        "start_date": "2023-05-23",
        "end_date": "2023-05-24"
    }

Run batch backtests with local config JSONs.

Accepts up to 10 config JSONs and runs them in parallel.
Returns a batch ID and individual job IDs.

Run backtest with exact CLI command (for testing/comparison).

This endpoint runs the exact same CLI command as would be executed manually,
including the --upload-gcs flag, to ensure 100% identical execution for log comparison.

Example:
    POST /api/v1/backtest/run-exact-cli
    {
        "config_gcs_path": "gs://execution-store-.../configs/V2/.../config.json",  # noqa: gs-uri
        "start_date": "2023-05-23T00:00:00Z",
        "end_date": "2023-05-23T00:05:00Z",
        "upload_gcs": true
    }

get.description:
Get status of a backtest job.

Returns current status, progress percentage, logs, and result path when complete.

Args:
    job_id: Job ID to get status for
    full_logs: If True, return all logs instead of just the last 20 lines (default: False)

post.description:
Trigger mass deployment via unified-trading-deployment-v2.

This endpoint calls the UTD-v2 CLI to deploy backtests across Cloud Run Jobs.
Uses sharding for parallel execution across many machines.

Example:
    POST /api/v1/backtest/mass-deploy
    {
        "config_folders": ["configs/V1/CEFI_BTC_momentum-macd/SCE/5M/"],
        "start_date": "2023-05-23",
        "end_date": "2023-05-25",
        "force": false
    }

Validate a backtest config.

Checks:
- Valid strategy_id format
- Valid config_id format
- Execution section uses new format
- Algorithm horizon <= strategy timeframe

Check upstream dependencies for a config.

Verifies:
- Strategy instructions exist for strategy_id/date
- Market tick data exists for instruments
- Instrument definitions exist for venues

get.description:
List available models from GCS model registry (ml-models-store bucket).

Reads model metadata from GCS path:
    model_registry/metadata/{model_id}/metadata.json

Returns mock data when CLOUD_MOCK_MODE=true.
Returns an empty list when GCS is unavailable (local/dev mode).

Get metadata for a specific model by model_id.

Reads from GCS path:
    model_registry/metadata/{model_id}/metadata.json

post.description:
Publish a deploy command to the PubSub ml-deploy topic.

The ml-inference-service consumes this topic and loads the model
into its in-memory model cache.

Returns a job_id that can be polled for completion status.

schema.title: Response Deploy Model Models Deploy Post

post.description:
Publish an undeploy command to the PubSub ml-deploy topic.

The ml-inference-service evicts the model from its in-memory cache.

schema.title: Response Undeploy Model Models  Model Id  Undeploy Post

get.description:
Return recent prediction records from BigQuery.

Reads from:
    {predictions_analytics_dataset}.{predictions_bq_table}

Results are ordered by prediction_timestamp DESC.
Returns mock data when CLOUD_MOCK_MODE=true.
Returns an empty list when BigQuery is unavailable (local/dev mode).

post.description: Record a new prediction. Persists in mock state when CLOUD_MOCK_MODE=true.

get.description: Liveness probe — always returns ok when the process is alive.
Readiness probe — returns ready when the service can handle requests.

List available features from the features GCS bucket.

Shows which features are ready per date/timeframe — used as a Phase 1
prerequisite check before triggering feature selection.

GCS path pattern: features/{service}/{date}/*.parquet
Phase 1 output:   feature_selection/{instrument}/{timeframe}/selected_features.json

Returns mock data when CLOUD_MOCK_MODE=true.
Returns one entry per (service, date, timeframe) combination found in GCS.
Stub: always returns [] until GCS reader is wired.

List all training experiments.

Sources:
- BQ ``ml_training`` dataset — run metadata and phase status
- GCS ``model_registry/metadata/`` — Phase 3 registry entries

Returns mock data when CLOUD_MOCK_MODE=true.
Stub: always returns [] until BQ reader is wired.

post.description: Create a new experiment. Persists in mock state when CLOUD_MOCK_MODE=true.

get.description:
Get experiment detail including all 3 pipeline phases.

Phase 1 GCS: feature_selection/{instrument}/{timeframe}/selected_features.json
Phase 2 GCS: hyperparam_tuning/{model_id}/training-period-{YYYY-MM}/best_params.json
Phase 3 GCS: models/{model_id}/model.joblib + model_registry/metadata/

Returns mock data when CLOUD_MOCK_MODE=true.
Stub: returns empty phases list until GCS/BQ readers are wired.

Get phase-specific detail for an experiment.

Phase 1 — Feature Selection:
    GCS: feature_selection/{instrument}/{timeframe}/selected_features.json
Phase 2 — Hyperparameter Tuning:
    GCS: hyperparam_tuning/{model_id}/training-period-{YYYY-MM}/best_params.json
Phase 3 — Final Model Training:
    GCS: models/{model_id}/model.joblib
    BQ:  ml_training dataset (metrics + run metadata)

Stub: returns phase envelope until GCS/BQ readers are wired.

List trained model registry entries from GCS model_registry/metadata/.

Returns mock data when CLOUD_MOCK_MODE=true.
Stub: always returns [] until GCS reader is wired.

post.description: Register a new trained model. Persists in mock state when CLOUD_MOCK_MODE=true.

get.description: Liveness probe — returns 200 when the process is running.

Readiness probe — returns 200 only when order recovery is complete.

Returns 503 with status=starting if recovery has not yet finished.
Returns 200 with status=ready once recovery is complete.

Readiness probe — verifies critical subsystems are initialised.

Checks:
- Auth and limiter are wired (startup event has completed).
Raises 503 if the service is not ready to handle requests.

get.description:
Compute VaR, CVaR, and optional Stress VaR for a returns series.

Phase 1: caller supplies `returns[]` in query params.
Phase 2: fetch from position-monitor returns history.

Args:
    client_id: Client identifier (logged with event).
    confidence: Confidence level in (0, 1). Default 0.99.
    horizon_days: Holding period. Default 1-day.
    scenario: Optional stress scenario name.
    returns: Daily P&L returns as decimals (e.g. -0.02 for -2%).

Returns:
    VaRResponse with var, cvar, stress_var, confidence, horizon_days, scenario.

parameters.name: scenario

schema.description: Optional stress scenario: GFC_2008, COVID_2020, CRYPTO_BLACK_THURSDAY_2020
schema.title: Scenario

parameters.description: Optional stress scenario: GFC_2008, COVID_2020, CRYPTO_BLACK_THURSDAY_2020

parameters.name: returns

schema.description: Daily P&L returns series (required; e.g. -0.02 = -2% loss)
schema.title: Returns

parameters.description: Daily P&L returns series (required; e.g. -0.02 = -2% loss)
